subject: Mathematics
title: Regular Tilings
description: A quick, practical guide to Regular Tilings.

hook.text: Can you tile your bathroom floor with just one shape and no gaps? Only three regular shapes work.

buildup.text: Equilateral triangles, squares, and regular hexagons are the only regular polygons that tile a flat plane.

discovery.text: The angles at each vertex must add up to exactly 360 degrees. Only those three achieve this.

twist.text: Regular pentagons leave gaps; regular octagons overlap. The math forbids them from tiling.

climax.text: This constraint, known since antiquity, limits floor designers and architects to this day.

punchline.text: Three shapes tile perfectly. No more, no less.

quiz.question: Which regular polygon CANNOT tile a flat plane?
